title: Iridescent Shell Ion Spectrometer
description: Users operate a futuristic ion-drive spectrometer to analyze the chemical composition of an iridescent abalone shell. By adjusting ion beam energy, focus aperture, and scan angle, they excite different molecular layers of the shell and observe spectral emission signatures in real time.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements an interactive ion spectrometer analyzing an abalone shell. Load both GLB assets using GLTFLoader: place IridescenceAbalone.glb at center as the specimen on a rotating sample platform, and PrimaryIonDrive.glb offset to the left as the ion beam emitter aimed at the shell. Camera: perspective at position (0, 3, 7) looking at origin. Lighting: one HemisphereLight (sky 0x8899ff, ground 0x443322, intensity 1.0), one DirectionalLight (0xffffff, intensity 2.0) from upper-right, and a PointLight (0x00ffcc, intensity 0) near the shell that activates during scans. Add an HTML overlay panel with these controls: (1) a 'Beam Energy' slider (1-10 keV, default 1), (2) a 'Focus Aperture' slider (0.1-2.0 mm, default 1.0), (3) a 'Scan Angle' slider (-45 to 45 degrees, default 0) that rotates the ion drive model around the Y-axis, (4) a 'Wavelength Filter' dropdown with options UV/Visible/IR, (5) a 'Start Scan' button, and (6) a 'Reset' button. When 'Start Scan' is clicked, validate that beam energy is above 3 keV and aperture is below 1.5 mm (show red warning text if invalid). On valid scan: animate a glowing cyan particle stream (use BufferGeometry points) from the ion drive nozzle to the shell surface over 1.5 seconds, then make the shell's material emissive color shift based on the wavelength filter selection (UV=violet 0x8800ff, Visible=green 0x00ff88, IR=red 0xff3300). The PointLight near the shell should pulse in matching color. Display a results panel showing detected elements (calcium carbonate for low energy, aragonite crystal for mid, nacre protein matrix for high energy). The sample platform should slowly auto-rotate. The scan angle slider must visibly rotate the ion drive model. Reset clears all emissions, results, and resets sliders. Use OrbitControls for camera interaction. Ensure all parameter combinations produce distinct visual feedback.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  beamEnergy: number,         // current ion beam energy in keV (1-10)
  focusAperture: number,         // aperture diameter in mm (0.1-2.0)
  scanAngle: number,         // ion drive rotation angle in degrees (-45 to 45)
  wavelengthFilter: string,         // selected spectral filter (UV, Visible, IR)
  isScanning: boolean,         // true when scan animation is active
  scanProgress: number,         // scan completion percentage (0-100)
  detectedPeaks: number,         // count of spectral emission peaks detected
  shellRotation: number,         // cumulative rotation of specimen platform in radians
  pointLightIntensity: number,         // current intensity of scan point light (0-3)
  scanCount: number,         // total number of completed scans
  lastScanValid: boolean,         // whether most recent scan met validation criteria
}
